title: podman 5.0.3 fails now with message pasta failed with exit code 1: couldn't set IPv6 route(s) in guest: no route to host
body:
### Issue Description

This seems very similar to the closed issue from a couple months ago #22192.

My configuration was bit more complex as I'm running a few containers in a pod.  The workaround from that was to use --net=pasta:-4 argument and that works for the containers that are not in the pod however, it doesn't work for the containers I try to run in a pod.

To simplify things I tried just doing the basics from that issue #22192.

So if I do
podman run --rm fedora:latest

The error is:
Error: pasta failed with exit code 1:
Couldn't set IPv6 route(s) in guest: No route to host

If I do:
podman run --net=pasta:-4 --rm fedora:latest

Then it works as expected.  I'm having issues because this workaround doesn't work for running containers inside pods apparently but as I started troubleshooting since I can't run a simple container I figured it was easiest to troubleshoot that problem and fixing that will probably fix the container inside of a pod issue.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Running up to date version of Manjaro
2. installed podman v5.0.3
3. installed passt v 2024-05-23-765eb0b-1
4. Run the following command - podman run --rm fedora:latest


### Describe the results you received

The container doesn't run and I receive the following error message:

Error: pasta failed with exit code 1:
Couldn't set IPv6 route(s) in guest: No route to host

### Describe the results you expected

The expected result is for the container to run without any errors

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Manjaro 24.0.0 with all updates up to 2024-5-18 update installed

Everything was working prior to this update using podman v5.0.2 and passt v2024-04-26 along with various older versions (I've been running this particular set of containers and pods for almost 2 years now).

### Additional information

Just let me know if I can provide any other details.  I don't really work with containers daily and I set all of this up almost 2 years ago and it has worked flawlessly until now.  So I'm having to refresh myself on things but I do have the original commands I used to create the pods, containers and then the systemd .service files I use to currently launch everything.  For right now I can only run the containers that are no inside of a pod by adding the --net=pasta:-4 argument to them.  Adding that argument to containers that are trying to start inside a pod just fails with 

ERRO[0000] Starting some container dependencies         
ERRO[0000] "pasta failed with exit code 1:\nCouldn't set IPv6 route(s) in guest: No route to host\n" 

That is the same error message with or without the --net=pasta:-4 argument

comments.body:
> I'm having issues because this workaround doesn't work for running containers inside pods apparently but as I started troubleshooting since I can't run a simple container I figured it was easiest to troubleshoot that problem and fixing that will probably fix the container inside of a pod issue.

You have to set the network option on the pod not the container when using pods in general unless you explicitly do not want to share the network namespace.

Can you share your ipv6 routes? You can hide the real addresses but subnet and interface information is likely needed to reproduce.

cc @sbrivio-rh @dgibson

Thank you for the super fast response @Luap99 .  First off while I thought I had properly passed the --net=pasta:-4 argument to my pod I clearly didn't (or typo'd it when I had tried because I just tested it again and that "fixed my pod" so my containers are working again.  Since they don't need ipv6 to function it is a perfectly fine workaround however, to further assist troubleshooting the cause of this because I can no longer run containers on my system without forcing ipv4 only.

I did a bit more digging and I'm thinking this issue is related to #22320 as I'm connected to a VPN.

My physical network configuration is a wireless connection to my router, and I'm also running a VPN  (ProtonVPN)

Here are my ipv6 routes

I'll tag the people you cc'd above.

The Proposed Fix by @sbrivio-rh **almost** works.

Procedure:
```
# Change to Sources Folder
root@MYHOST# cd /usr/src

# Clone Repository
root@MYHOST# git clone https://passt.top/passt

# Change Folder
root@MYHOST# cd passt/

# Copy the Patch outlined by @sbrivio-rh into netlink.patch
root@MYHOST# nano netlink.patch
(paste the contents here)

# Apply Patch
# In my case I need to install patch first
root@MYHOST# dnf install patch
root@MYHOST# patch -p1 < netlink.patch

# Build - First Attempt
root@MYHOST# make

# Test (as USER)
podman@MYHOST$ /usr/src/passt/pasta --config-net --debug

# It will fail, in my case on the nat46 route for snid
# Failed to copy route, dst: 64:ff9b:1::1:0:0 gw: 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:0001::1:66

# Mark patch as already applied
root@MYHOST# mv netlink.patch netlink.patch.already_applied

# Replace this line: if (rc < 0 && rc != -ENETUNREACH && rc != -EEXIST) {
# With this line: if (rc < 0 && rc != -ENETUNREACH && rc != -EHOSTUNREACH && rc != -EEXIST) {

# Build - Second Attempt
root@MYHOST# make

# Test as USER
# Now no Errors (without --debug NOTHING is displayed)
podman@MYHOST$ /usr/src/passt/pasta --config-net --debug
```

       valid_lft 86338sec preferred_lft 14338sec
    inet6 fe80::XXXX:XXXX:XXXX:XXXX/64 scope link 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever

EDIT 3: `compose.yml` file for `podman-compose`

```
services:
  whoami-application:
    container_name: whoami-application
    environment:
      WHOAMI_PORT_NUMBER: '8080'
    image: traefik/whoami
    network_mode: service:whoami-caddy
    pull_policy: missing
    restart: unless-stopped
  whoami-caddy:
    container_name: whoami-caddy
    env_file:
    - .env
    environment:
      CADDY_DOCKER_CADDYFILE_PATH: /etc/caddy/Caddyfile
    image: caddy:latest
    network_mode: pasta:--ipv6-only,-t,2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:0001:0000:0000:ff15:0001/80,-t,2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:0001:0000:0000:ff15:0001/443,-u,2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:0001:0000:0000:ff15:0001/443
    ports:
    - host_ip: '[2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:0001:0000:0000:ff15:0001]'
      protocol: tcp
      published: 80
      target: 80
    - host_ip: '[2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:0001:0000:0000:ff15:0001]'
      protocol: tcp
      published: 443
      target: 443
    - host_ip: '[2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:0001:0000:0000:ff15:0001]'
      protocol: udp
      published: 443
      target: 443
    pull_policy: missing
    restart: unless-stopped
    security_opt:
    - no-new-privileges:true
    volumes:
    - ./Caddyfile:/etc/caddy/Caddyfile:ro,z
    - ~/containers/data/whoami-caddy:/data:rw,z
    - ~/containers/log/whoami-caddy:/var/log:rw,z
    - ~/containers/config/whoami-caddy:/config:rw,z
    - ~/containers/certificates/letsencrypt:/certificates:ro,z
```

The `pasta` one-liner in `compose.yml` should be:
```
network_mode: pasta:--ipv6-only,-t,2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:0001:0000:0000:ff15:0001/80,-t,2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:0001:0000:0000:ff15:0001/443,-u,2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:0001:0000:0000:ff15:0001/443
```

EDIT 4: 
Correct me if I'm wrong but since `--config-net` should be always included, that should translate to:
```
pasta --config-net --ipv6-only -t 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:0001:0000:0000:ff15:0001/80 -t 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:0001:0000:0000:ff15:0001/443 -u 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:0001:0000:0000:ff15:0001/443
```

References:
- https://github.com/luckylinux/podman/blob/tutorial-ipv6-pasta/docs/tutorials/podman_pasta_ipv6_with_snid_ipv4.md

EDIT 6: the `noprefixroute` seems to automatically be added by NetworkManager, see https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/NetworkManager/NetworkManager/-/issues/1452. NetworkManager is enabled by default on Fedora 40 (and earlier Versions). Not sure how to configure the Network otherwise.

Strangely enough the same `noprefixroute` occurs on ALL Interface Addresses MINUS 1 on my Ubuntu Desktop (NOT managed by NetworkManager).

EDIT 7: Fix until Fedora will have the updated Version in its Repositories.
```
# Git Commit prior to applying Patch + One-Line fix
root@MYHOST# git show
commit 4070bac7a48f92041617363a5e94ab760ba72073

# Install on System-Level
root@MYHOST# cd /usr/src/passt
root@MYHOST# make install

# Print Version 
root@MYHOST# pasta --version
pasta 2024_06_07.8a83b53-9-g4070bac
```

Fixed in [2024_06_24.1ee2eca](https://archives.passt.top/passt-user/20240624210651.61ce77af@elisabeth/), and matching Fedora 40 [update](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2024-4632bfa865). Updated Arch Linux package should follow soon.
